req.title: Immunization record (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall record an Immunization with administered date not in the future. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests: Immunization Administered Date Validation

```python
"""
Pytest tests for requirement:
  "A clinician shall record an Immunization with administered date not in the future."

Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements are explicitly covered.

Assumptions / Design Decisions
--------------------------------
- The system under test exposes a function (or service layer method):
      record_immunization(payload: dict) -> ImmunizationRecord
  which raises ValueError (or a domain-specific exception) when the
  administered date is in the future.
- Date/time values are ISO-8601 strings or datetime objects; the system
  resolves "now" internally via a clock that can be injected / mocked.
- Numeric boundary fields tested: dose_number, dose_quantity_value.
- All times are UTC unless the test explicitly probes timezone behaviour.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock
from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo  # Python 3.9+; use pytz if on 3.8

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Production code import (adjust path to your project layout)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from immunization.service import record_immunization   # noqa: E402
from immunization.exceptions import (                  # noqa: E402
    FutureDateError,
    InvalidDoseNumberError,
    InvalidDoseQuantityError,
    MissingFieldError,
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers / constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
FIXED_NOW = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)   # "wall clock" used in all mocks

VALID_PAYLOAD = {
    "patient_id": "patient-001",
    "vaccine_code": "208",            # CVX code – COVID-19
    "lot_number": "LOT-XYZ",
    "administered_date": FIXED_NOW.isoformat(),
    "dose_number": 1,
    "dose_quantity_value": 0.5,
    "dose_quantity_unit": "mL",
    "administering_clinician_id": "clinician-007",
    "site": "left-deltoid",
    "route": "intramuscular",
}


def _payload(**overrides):
    """Return a copy of VALID_PAYLOAD with the given overrides applied."""
    p = VALID_PAYLOAD.copy()
    p.update(overrides)
    return p


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def freeze_clock():
    """
    Freeze the internal clock used by the service to FIXED_NOW so that
    every test runs against a deterministic 'now'.
    """
    with patch("immunization.service.get_current_utc_time", return_value=FIXED_NOW):
        yield


# ===========================================================================
# 1. HAPPY-PATH TESTS — valid administered dates
# ===========================================================================
class TestValidAdministeredDates:

    def test_administered_date_equals_now_exactly(self):
        """Boundary: administered_date == now should be accepted (not in the future)."""
        payload = _payload(administered_date=FIXED_NOW.isoformat())
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result.administered_date == FIXED_NOW

    def test_administered_date_one_second_before_now(self):
        """Boundary: now - 1 second must be accepted."""
        past = FIXED_NOW - timedelta(seconds=1)
        payload = _payload(administered_date=past.isoformat())
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result.administered_date == past

    def test_administered_date_one_minute_before_now(self):
        past = FIXED_NOW - timedelta(minutes=1)
        payload = _payload(administered_date=past.isoformat())
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result.administered_date == past

    def test_administered_date_one_hour_before_now(self):
        past = FIXED_NOW - timedelta(hours=1)
        payload = _payload(administered_date=past.isoformat())
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result.administered_date == past

    def test_administered_date_one_day_before_now(self):
        past = FIXED_NOW - timedelta(days=1)
        payload = _payload(administered_date=past.isoformat())
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result.administered_date == past

    def test_administered_date_one_year_in_the_past(self):
        past = FIXED_NOW - timedelta(days=365)
        payload = _payload(administered_date=past.isoformat())
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result.administered_date == past

    def test_administered_date_epoch_zero(self):
        """Extreme past boundary: Unix epoch."""
        epoch = datetime(1970, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        payload = _payload(administered_date=epoch.isoformat())
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result.administered_date == epoch

    def test_administered_date_with_microseconds_in_the_past(self):
        """Microsecond-level precision: now - 1 µs must be accepted."""
        past = FIXED_NOW - timedelta(microseconds=1)
        payload = _payload(administered_date=past.isoformat())
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result.administered_date == past

    def test_administered_date_with_non_utc_timezone_equivalent_to_past(self):
        """
        A date expressed in a non-UTC timezone that is still in the past
        when converted to UTC must be accepted.
        Eastern Daylight Time = UTC-4; 07:00 EDT == 11:00 UTC < 12:00 UTC (FIXED_NOW).
        """
        edt = ZoneInfo("America/New_York")
        past_edt = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 7, 0, 0, tzinfo=edt)   # == 11:00 UTC
        payload = _payload(administered_date=past_edt.isoformat())
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result.administered_date.utcoffset() is not None   # tz-aware stored

    def test_administered_date_end_of_previous_day(self):
        """23:59:59 UTC of the previous day should be accepted."""
        end_of_yesterday = FIXED_NOW.replace(hour=23, minute=59, second=59) - timedelta(days=1)
        payload = _payload(administered_date=end_of_yesterday.isoformat())
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result.administered_date < FIXED_NOW


# ===========================================================================
# 2. REJECTION TESTS — future administered dates
# ===========================================================================
class TestFutureAdministeredDates:

    def test_administered_date_one_second_in_future_raises(self):
        """Boundary: now + 1 second must be rejected."""
        future = FIXED_NOW + timedelta(seconds=1)
        with pytest.raises(FutureDateError):
            record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=future.isoformat()))

    def test_administered_date_one_microsecond_in_future_raises(self):
        """Boundary: now + 1 µs must be rejected (strict greater-than check)."""
        future = FIXED_NOW + timedelta(microseconds=1)
        with pytest.raises(FutureDateError):
            record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=future.isoformat()))

    def test_administered_date_one_minute_in_future_raises(self):
        future = FIXED_NOW + timedelta(minutes=1)
        with pytest.raises(FutureDateError):
            record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=future.isoformat()))

    def test_administered_date_one_hour_in_future_raises(self):
        future = FIXED_NOW + timedelta(hours=1)
        with pytest.raises(FutureDateError):
            record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=future.isoformat()))

    def test_administered_date_one_day_in_future_raises(self):
        future = FIXED_NOW + timedelta(days=1)
        with pytest.raises(FutureDateError):
            record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=future.isoformat()))

    def test_administered_date_far_future_raises(self):
        future = FIXED_NOW + timedelta(days=3650)   # ~10 years
        with pytest.raises(FutureDateError):
            record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=future.isoformat()))

    def test_future_date_error_message_contains_administered_date(self):
        """The error must communicate the offending date to the caller."""
        future = FIXED_NOW + timedelta(hours=2)
        with pytest.raises(FutureDateError) as exc_info:
            record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=future.isoformat()))
        assert future.isoformat() in str(exc_info.value)

    def test_administered_date_non_utc_timezone_future_when_converted_raises(self):
        """
        A local time that appears past locally but is future in UTC must be rejected.
        UTC+14 (Line Islands): 02:01 on 2024-06-16 UTC+14 == 12:01 UTC > FIXED_NOW.
        """
        utc_plus_14 = ZoneInfo("Etc/GMT-14")
        future_local = datetime(2024, 6, 16, 2, 1, 0, tzinfo=utc_plus_14)   # 12:01 UTC
        with pytest.raises(FutureDateError):
            record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=future_local.isoformat()))

    def test_administered_date_midnight_start_of_tomorrow_raises(self):
        """00:00:00 UTC of the next day must be rejected."""
        start_of_tomorrow = (FIXED_NOW + timedelta(days=1)).replace(
            hour=0, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0
        )
        with pytest.raises(FutureDateError):
            record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=start_of_tomorrow.isoformat()))


# ===========================================================================
# 3. TIMESTAMP FORMAT / PARSE BOUNDARY TESTS
# ===========================================================================
class TestTimestampFormatBoundaries:

    def test_date_only_string_treated_as_start_of_day_past(self):
        """
        A date-only value (no time component) for yesterday should default
        to 00:00:00 UTC and be accepted.
        """
        yesterday_date = (FIXED_NOW - timedelta(days=1)).strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
        payload = _payload(administered_date=yesterday_date)
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result is not None

    def test_date_only_string_for_today_treated_as_start_of_day_accepted(self):
        """
        A date-only value for today (00:00:00 UTC) is in the past relative
        to FIXED_NOW (12:00:00 UTC) and must be accepted.
        """
        today_date = FIXED_NOW.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
        payload = _payload(administered_date=today_date)
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result is not None

    def test_date_only_string_for_tomorrow_raises(self):
        """Date-only value for tomorrow must be rejected."""
        tomorrow_date = (FIXED_NOW + timedelta(days=1)).strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
        with pytest.raises(FutureDateError):
            record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=tomorrow_date))

    def test_invalid_date_string_raises_value_error(self):
        """Malformed date strings must raise a ValueError, not be silently accepted."""
        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            record_immunization(_payload(administered_date="not-a-date"))

    def test_empty_administered_date_raises_missing_field_error(self):
        with pytest.raises(MissingFieldError):
            record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=""))

    def test_none_administered_date_raises_missing_field_error(self):
        with pytest.raises(MissingFieldError):
            record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=None))

    def test_administered_date_missing_key_raises_missing_field_error(self):
        payload = VALID_PAYLOAD.copy()
        del payload["administered_date"]
        with pytest.raises(MissingFieldError):
            record_immunization(payload)

    def test_administered_date_unix_timestamp_integer_past(self):
        """Some callers may pass a Unix timestamp integer; past value must be accepted."""
        past_ts = int(FIXED_NOW.timestamp()) - 60   # 60 seconds ago
        payload = _payload(administered_date=past_ts)
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result is not None

    def test_administered_date_unix_timestamp_integer_future_raises(self):
        future_ts = int(FIXED_NOW.timestamp()) + 60
        with pytest.raises(FutureDateError):
            record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=future_ts))

    def test_administered_date_naive_datetime_rejected(self):
        """
        A timezone-naive datetime string must be rejected or raise an error
        because the server cannot safely determine whether it is in the future.
        """
        naive = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 12, 0, 0)   # no tzinfo
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="timezone"):
            record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=naive.isoformat()))


# ===========================================================================
# 4. NUMERIC BOUNDARY TESTS — dose_number
# ===========================================================================
class TestDoseNumberBoundaries:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("dose_number", [1, 2, 3, 10, 100])
    def test_valid_dose_numbers_accepted(self, dose_number):
        payload = _payload(dose_number=dose_number)
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result.dose_number == dose_number

    def test_dose_number_zero_raises(self):
        """Zero is not a valid dose number; minimum is 1."""
        with pytest.raises(InvalidDoseNumberError):
            record_immunization(_payload(dose_number=0))

    def test_dose_number_negative_raises(self):
        with pytest.raises(InvalidDoseNumberError):
            record_immunization(_payload(dose_number=-1))

    def test_dose_number_large_negative_raises(self):
        with pytest.raises(InvalidDoseNumberError):
            record_immunization(_payload(dose_number=-9999))

    def test_dose_number_float_raises(self):
        """Dose numbers must be whole integers."""
        with pytest.raises((InvalidDoseNumberError, TypeError)):
            record_immunization(_payload(dose_number=1.5))

    def test_dose_number_none_raises(self):
        with pytest.raises((InvalidDoseNumberError, MissingFieldError)):
            record_immunization(_payload(dose_number=None))

    def test_dose_number_string_digit_coerced_or_raises(self):
        """
        If the system coerces "1" → 1, that is acceptable.
        If it raises, that is also acceptable. Either way it must not silently
        store a non-integer value.
        """
        try:
            result = record_immunization(_payload(dose_number="1"))
            assert result.dose_number == 1
        except (InvalidDoseNumberError, TypeError, ValueError):
            pass   # rejection is equally valid

    def test_dose_number_max_boundary_accepted(self):
        """Upper practical boundary (e.g., 999) must be accepted."""
        result = record_immunization(_payload(dose_number=999))
        assert result.dose_number == 999

    def test_dose_number_overflow_raises(self):
        """Extremely large integers beyond business rules must raise."""
        with pytest.raises(InvalidDoseNumberError):
            record_immunization(_payload(dose_number=10_000))


# ===========================================================================
# 5. NUMERIC BOUNDARY TESTS — dose_quantity_value
# ===========================================================================
class TestDoseQuantityValueBoundaries:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("qty", [0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 5.0])
    def test_valid_dose_quantities_accepted(self, qty):
        payload = _payload(dose_quantity_value=qty)
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result.dose_quantity_value == pytest.approx(qty)

    def test_dose_quantity_zero_raises(self):
        """Zero volume administered is clinically invalid."""
        with pytest.raises(InvalidDoseQuantityError):
            record_immunization(_payload(dose_quantity_value=0))

    def test_dose_quantity_negative_raises(self):
        with pytest.raises(InvalidDoseQuantityError):
            record_immunization(_payload(dose_quantity_value=-0.5))

    def test_dose_quantity_very_small_positive_accepted(self):
        """Smallest clinically plausible dose (0.01 mL) must be accepted."""
        result = record_immunization(_payload(dose_quantity_value=0.01))
        assert result.dose_quantity_value == pytest.approx(0.01)

    def test_dose_quantity_extremely_small_positive_raises(self):
        """Sub-clinical quantity below system minimum (< 0.01 mL) must be rejected."""
        with pytest.raises(InvalidDoseQuantityError):
            record_immunization(_payload(dose_quantity_value=0.0001))

    def test_dose_quantity_at_max_boundary_accepted(self):
        """Maximum single-dose volume (e.g., 10 mL) must be accepted."""
        result = record_immunization(_payload(dose_quantity_value=10.0))
        assert result.dose_quantity_value == pytest.approx(10.0)

    def test_dose_quantity_above_max_boundary_raises(self):
        """Volume exceeding clinical maximum must be rejected."""
        with pytest.raises(InvalidDoseQuantityError):
            record_immunization(_payload(dose_quantity_value=10.1))

    def test_dose_quantity_none_raises(self):
        with pytest.raises((InvalidDoseQuantityError, MissingFieldError)):
            record_immunization(_payload(dose_quantity_value=None))

    def test_dose_quantity_string_non_numeric_raises(self):
        with pytest.raises((InvalidDoseQuantityError, TypeError, ValueError)):
            record_immunization(_payload(dose_quantity_value="half"))

    def test_dose_quantity_integer_one_accepted(self):
        """Integer 1 must be coerced to float 1.0 and accepted."""
        result = record_immunization(_payload(dose_quantity_value=1))
        assert result.dose_quantity_value == pytest.approx(1.0)


# ===========================================================================
# 6. COMBINED BOUNDARY TESTS (date + numeric fields simultaneously)
# ===========================================================================
class TestCombinedBoundaries:

    def test_boundary_date_and_boundary_dose_number_accepted(self):
        """now exactly + minimum valid dose number (1) must both be accepted."""
        result = record_immunization(
            _payload(administered_date=FIXED_NOW.isoformat(), dose_number=1)
        )
        assert result.administered_date == FIXED_NOW
        assert result.dose_number == 1

    def test_future_date_overrides_valid_numeric_fields(self):
        """
        Even with valid dose fields, a future date must cause rejection.
        The date violation takes precedence.
        """
        future = FIXED_NOW + timedelta(seconds=1)
        with pytest.raises(FutureDateError):
            record_immunization(
                _payload(
                    administered_date=future.isoformat(),
                    dose_number=1,
                    dose_quantity_value=0.5,
                )
            )

    def test_valid_date_with_invalid_dose_number_raises(self):
        """Past date does not suppress dose_number validation."""
        past = FIXED_NOW - timedelta(hours=1)
        with pytest.raises(InvalidDoseNumberError):
            record_immunization(
                _payload(administered_date=past.isoformat(), dose_number=0)
            )

    def test_valid_date_with_invalid_dose_quantity_raises(self):
        """Past date does not suppress dose_quantity_value validation."""
        past = FIXED_NOW - timedelta(hours=1)
        with pytest.raises(InvalidDoseQuantityError):
            record_immunization(
                _payload(administered_date=past.isoformat(), dose_quantity_value=-1.0)
            )

    def test_all_boundary_values_at_minimum_accepted(self):
        """All fields at their minimum valid boundary simultaneously."""
        past = FIXED_NOW - timedelta(seconds=1)   # just past
        result = record_immunization(
            _payload(
                administered_date=past.isoformat(),
                dose_number=1,
                dose_quantity_value=0.01,
            )
        )
        assert result is not None

    def test_all_boundary_values_at_maximum_accepted(self):
        """All fields at their maximum valid boundary simultaneously."""
        result = record_immunization(
            _payload(
                administered_date=FIXED_NOW.isoformat(),
                dose_number=999,
                dose_quantity_value=10.0,
            )
        )
        assert result is not None


# ===========================================================================
# 7. CLOCK / CONCURRENCY EDGE CASES
# ===========================================================================
class TestClockEdgeCases:

    def test_clock_ticks_forward_between_validation_and_storage(self):
        """
        Simulate a race where time moves forward between the guard check
        and the database write. The record should still be stored because
        the validation timestamp (at call time) was ≤ now.
        """
        call_times = iter([FIXED_NOW, FIXED_NOW + timedelta(seconds=5)])
        with patch(
            "immunization.service.get_current_utc_time", side_effect=call_times
        ):
            result = record_immunization(
                _payload(administered_date=FIXED_NOW.isoformat())
            )
        assert result is not None

    def test_leap_second_boundary_accepted(self):
        """
        2016-12-31T23:59:60Z is a historic leap second.
        The system should either normalise it to 00:00:00 of the next day
        or reject it with a ValueError—never silently corrupt it.
        """
        leap_second_str = "2016-12-31T23:59:60+00:00"
        try:
            result = record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=leap_second_str))
            # If accepted, ensure stored date is sane
            assert result.administered_date < FIXED_NOW
        except (ValueError, FutureDateError):
            pass   # either outcome is acceptable

    def test_dst_spring_forward_gap_raises_or_normalises(self):
        """
        2024-03-10T02:30:00 does not exist in US/Eastern (spring forward).
        Must not be silently accepted as a valid past date.
        """
        us_eastern = ZoneInfo("America/New_York")
        non_existent = "2024-03-10T02:30:00-05:00"   # gap hour
        try:
            result = record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=non_existent))
            # If the system normalises, the stored value must still be in the past
            assert result.administered_date < FIXED_NOW
        except (ValueError, FutureDateError):
            pass


# ===========================================================================
# 8. PARAMETRIZED SWEEP — systematic boundary table
# ===========================================================================
FUTURE_DELTAS = [
    timedelta(microseconds=1),
    timedelta(seconds=1),
    timedelta(minutes=1),
    timedelta(hours=1),
    timedelta(days=1),
    timedelta(weeks=1),
    timedelta(days=365),
]

PAST_OR_NOW_DELTAS = [
    timedelta(0),                    # exactly now
    timedelta(microseconds=-1),      # 1 µs ago
    timedelta(seconds=-1),
    timedelta(minutes=-1),
    timedelta(hours=-1),
    timedelta(days=-1),
    timedelta(weeks=-1),
    timedelta(days=-365),
]


@pytest.mark.parametrize("delta", FUTURE_DELTAS, ids=lambda d: f"future+{d}")
def test_parametrized_future_dates_rejected(delta):
    future = FIXED_NOW + delta
    with pytest.raises(FutureDateError):
        record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=future.isoformat()))


@pytest.mark.parametrize("delta", PAST_OR_NOW_DELTAS, ids=lambda d: f"past_or_now{d}")
def test_parametrized_past_or_now_dates_accepted(delta):
    administered = FIXED_NOW + delta
    result = record_immunization(_payload(administered_date=administered.isoformat()))
    assert result is not None
    assert result.administered_date <= FIXED_NOW
```

---

## What Each Section Covers

| Section | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| **1 – Valid dates** | Dates at, before, and well before `now`; epoch; microsecond precision; non-UTC timezones that resolve to the past |
| **2 – Future dates** | +1 µs, +1 s, +1 min, +1 hr, +1 day, far future; non-UTC timezone that resolves to the future; error message content |
| **3 – Timestamp formats** | Date-only strings, Unix integer timestamps, naive (tz-free) datetimes, `None`, empty string, malformed strings |
| **4 – `dose_number` numerics** | Min (1), zero, negative, float, `None`, string digit, max boundary, overflow |
| **5 – `dose_quantity_value` numerics** | Min positive, zero, negative, sub-clinical, max boundary, above max, `None`, non-numeric string |
| **6 – Combined** | Future date + valid numerics still rejected; past date doesn't suppress numeric errors; all-min and all-max simultaneously valid |
| **7 – Clock edge cases** | Clock advancing between validation and storage; leap second; DST spring-forward gap |
| **8 – Parametrized sweep** | Systematic table-driven coverage of every meaningful time delta in both directions |

# Pytest Tests: Immunization Administered Date Validation

```python
"""
Pytest tests for requirement:
  "A clinician shall record an Immunization with administered date not in the future."

Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements are explicitly covered.

Assumptions / Design Decisions
--------------------------------
- The system under test exposes a function (or service layer method):
      record_immunization(payload: dict) -> ImmunizationRecord
  which raises ValueError (or a domain-specific exception) when the
  administered date is in the future.
- Date/time values are ISO-8601 strings or datetime objects; the system
  resolves "now" internally via a clock that can be injected / mocked.
- Numeric boundary fields tested: dose_number, dose_quantity_value.
- All times are UTC unless the test explicitly probes timezone behaviour.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock
from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo  # Python 3.9+; use pytz if on 3.8

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Production code import (adjust path to your project layout)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from immunization.service import record_immunization   # noqa: E402
from immunization.exceptions import (                  # noqa: E402
    FutureDateError,
    InvalidDoseNumberError,
    InvalidDoseQuantityError,
    MissingFieldError,
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers / constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
FIXED_NOW = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)   # "wall clock" used in all mocks

VALID_PAYLOAD = {
    "patient_id": "patient-001",
    "vaccine_code": "208",            # CVX code – COVID-19
    "lot_number": "LOT-XYZ",
    "administered_date": FIXED_NOW.isoformat(),
    "dose_number": 1,
    "dose_quantity_value": 0.5,
    "dose_quantity_unit": "mL",
    "administering_clinician_id": "clinician-007",
    "site": "left-deltoid",
    "route": "intramuscular",
}


def _payload(**overrides):
    """Return a copy of VALID_PAYLOAD with the given overrides applied."""
    p = VALID_PAYLOAD.copy()
    p.update(overrides)
    return p


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def freeze_clock():
    """
    Freeze the internal clock used by the service to FIXED_NOW so that
    every test runs against a deterministic 'now'.
    """
    with patch("immunization.service.get_current_utc_time", return_value=FIXED_NOW):
        yield


# ===========================================================================
# 1. HAPPY-PATH TESTS — valid administered dates
# ===========================================================================
class TestValidAdministeredDates:

    def test_administered_date_equals_now_exactly(self):
        """Boundary: administered_date == now should be accepted (not in the future)."""
        payload = _payload(administered_date=FIXED_NOW.isoformat())
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result.administered_date == FIXED_NOW

    def test_administered_date_one_second_before_now(self):
        """Boundary: now - 1 second must be accepted."""
        past = FIXED_NOW - timedelta(seconds=1)
        payload = _payload(administered_date=past.isoformat())
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result.administered_date == past

    def test_administered_date_one_minute_before_now(self):
        past = FIXED_NOW - timedelta(minutes=1)
        payload = _payload(administered_date=past.isoformat())
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result.administered_date == past

    def test_administered_date_one_hour_before_now(self):
        past = FIXED_NOW - timedelta(hours=1)
        payload = _payload(administered_date=past.isoformat())
        result = record_immunization(payload)
        assert result.admin
